page.title: Osteoporosis — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Osteoporosis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Osteoporosis: 142 alterations and 221 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Nigella sativa and health outcomes: An overview of systematic reviews and meta-analyses.
The present study demonstrated that n-3 PUFA supplementation might not have a significant effect on bone mineral density or bone metabolism markers.
Effect of Tai Chi exercise on bone health and fall prevention in postmenopausal women.
Efficacy of vitamin K2 in the prevention and treatment of postmenopausal osteoporosis.
Greater dietary vitamin C intake was associated with a lower risk of hip fracture and osteoporosis
High-dose vitamin K supplementation reduces fracture incidence in postmenopausal women.
Long-term use of warfarin was associated with osteoporotic fractures, at least in men with atrial fibrillation.
Low serum level of magnesium seems to be a risk factor for osteoporosis among the postmenopausal women.
Naringin had been shown to promote bone formation in OVX rats.
Puerarin showed upregulating effects on bone mass in ovariectomy-induced postmenopausal osteoporosis.
Soy isoflavones prevent bone resorption and loss.
Tai chi is effective in improving balance of older adults and in the absence of other interventions, reduces falls in the nonfrail elderly.
The efficacy and safety of ipriflavone in postmenopausal women with osteopenia or osteoporosis.
There was an association between the increase of at least one serving of fruits and vegetables per day and decreases in the risk of fractures.
This present systematic review indicated that acupuncture could be an effective therapy for treating osteoporosis.
Vitamin K1 and K2 exhibit anti-fracture activity.
Treatment of osteoporosis with a modified zeolite shows beneficial effects in an osteoporotic rat model and a human clinical trial .
A black cohosh extract is equipotent to conjugated estrogens in reducing menopausal symptoms and bone markers, without adverse effects.
A specific exercise program targeting osteoporosis improves the condition of postmenopausal women with low bone density.
Air pollution and risk of 32 health conditions: outcome-wide analyses in a population-based prospective cohort.
Alcohol consumption moderated the association between levels of high blood lead or total urinary arsenic and bone loss.
Alendronate is associated with injury and ulceration of the gastric mucosa.
Alendronate use is associated with low-energy femoral shaft fractures.
Although hip bone mineral density is strongly related to hip fracture risk in elderly white women, other clinical risk factors also are independent predictors of long-term risk.
Ambient air pollution was associated with lower bone mineral content in a young adult population in a peri-urban area of South India.
Arimidex therapy contributes in the decline of bone mineral density in early breast cancer patients.
Artemisia princeps as a promising therapeutic option for the improvement of postmenopausal osteoporosis.
Ascorbic acid may modify the composition of gut microbiota which plays a role in the pathogenesis of an inflammatory bowel disease.
Associations of ultra-processed food intake with bone mineral density and fractures in the UK Biobank.
Beer drinking contributes to higher bone density as a result of the phytoestrogen content (hops) of this alcoholic beverage.
Bisphosphonate use in chronic kidney disease is associated wtih adynamic bone disease.
Bisphosphonates used for osteoporosis in patients with cystic fibrosis increases bone mineral density without increasing bone quality/strength, while causing bone pain and flu-like symptoms.
Blackcurrants reduce the risk of postmenopausal osteoporosis.

Bone mineral density is a significant prognostic factor for postmenopausal Caucasian women with breast cancer. Low bone mineral density is associated with a lower local and distant rate of recurrence.
Calcium supplementation (without vitamin D) is associated with increased risk of bone fracture.
Calcium supplementation may actually increase the risk of bone fracture.
Carotenoids such as lycopene have a protective effect on bone health.
Children and adolescents with severe mental illness need vitamin D supplementation regardless of disease or treatment.
Clinical efficacy instant goat milk in the complex therapy and prevention of osteoporosis in patients with rheumatoid arthritis.
Clinical evaluation of a defined zeolite-clinoptilolite supplementation effect on the selected blood parameters of patients.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Bone density decline of 1-3%/year in postmenopausal women without treatment; fracture risk increases exponentially with T-score decline; no spontaneous remission
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Bisphosphonates: 40-50% reduction in vertebral fractures, 20-40% hip fracture reduction; romosozumab (anti-sclerostin): 73% vertebral fracture reduction at 12 months (ARCH trial); abaloparatide/teriparatide (anabolic): BMD increase 9-12% at lumbar spine over 18 months
remission.gap_size: Very large — post-fracture care gap is stark: only 20-30% of patients with fragility fracture receive osteoporosis treatment within 1 year
remission.barrier_detail: The 'post-fracture care gap' is one of the most documented practice failures in medicine; the majority of fragility fracture patients leave hospital without osteoporosis diagnosis or treatment; fracture liaison services (FLS) fix this but are not universally implemented; anabolic therapies (romosozumab, teriparatide) drastically underused vs anti-resorptives
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — NOF/ASBMR guidelines recommend anabolic therapy first-line in very high-risk patients (T-score <-3.0 or prior hip/vertebral fracture); romosozumab approved 2019; 2016 GIOP guidelines updated; bisphosphonates standard since mid-1990s
remission.outdated_treatments: Calcitonin nasal spray (evidence weak, potential cancer signal; largely abandoned in guidelines but still used); HRT as primary osteoporosis therapy without fracture risk stratification; calcium supplements >1000mg/day without dietary assessment (possible CV risk signal from supplemental calcium alone)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Resistance and weight-bearing exercise (osteogenic loading — high-impact stimuli); vibration therapy (low-magnitude mechanical signals — some trial data); dietary protein optimisation; fracture liaison services (FLS) achieving 40-50% secondary fracture reduction
remission.frontier_signal: Setrusumab (anti-sclerostin for OI and osteoporosis — Phase 3); denosumab 6-monthly subcutaneous (FREEDOM trial); sequential anabolic-antiresorptive therapy protocols optimisation; AI-based FRAX risk stratification tools
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
